text: The surgeon observes intense adherence in the surgical field, noting the pale appearance of the tissue. A large amount of liquid, identified as pus, is present, and the assistant is asked to wash the area.

The surgeon performs a maneuver to manipulate the appendix, positioning it to the side. They check for bleeding and observe a line indicating where to make an incision. The base of the appendix is visible, confirming appendicitis. The surgeon notes the difficulty in removing the appendix.
